how to play Indian poker on Android.response:
If you want to **play Indian poker on Android**, the easiest option is to install a dedicated app like **Indian poker** from Google Play, which says it lets you play Indian poker with playing cards and is intended for multiple people.[2]

If you want the **real-world rules** of Indian poker, the standard version is: each player gets one card face down, then places it on their forehead so everyone else can see it but they cannot see their own card; there is one betting round, then a showdown, and the highest card wins the pot.[7][6] Some rule sets also rank suits, with **spades high** and **clubs low**, but that is a variation rather than the only standard.[7]

If you meant a **mobile game app**, here are the relevant Android options from the results:
- **Indian poker** — a Google Play app for playing Indian poker with playing cards; it is described as intended for multiple people.[2]
- **Teen Patti: Indian Poker** — a card-game app listed for Android that includes bonus options and play with friends.[1]

To play in the app or with friends, the basic flow is:
- Deal one card to each player.[7]
- Do not look at your own card.[7]
- Put the card on your forehead so others can see it.[7][6]
- Take one betting round.[7][6]
- Reveal cards and award the pot to the highest card.[7]

If you want, I can also give you a **quick step-by-step for setting it up on Android** or **the simplest beginner strategy**.
